assertions.reason: Targeted Vitest evidence confirms checkout TTL default is 60s and timeout reaping cancels runs plus increments pickupFailCount, but the lease transitions to state=expired (lease_expired event) rather than being released as asserted.
Current Vitest suite does not exercise activity-triggered lease renewal via comments, status changes, or explicit keepalive requests; renewal is only directly tested through manual renewLease calls.

commands.command: pnpm vitest server/src/__tests__/lease-manager.test.ts -t "has checkout TTL of 60 seconds|full lifecycle: grant → renew → release"
commands.observation: 2 targeted tests passed (43 skipped). Confirms 60s checkout TTL constant and explicit renew/release lease lifecycle behavior.

commands.command: pnpm vitest server/src/__tests__/stale-run-reaper.test.ts -t "expires and cancels a run whose lease is still granted but past expiresAt|increments pickupFailCount when run never checked out the issue"
commands.observation: 2 targeted tests passed (17 skipped). Confirms stale lease timeout causes run cancellation, lease expiration, and pickupFailCount increment without checkout.

frictions.description: Targeted test output is noisy due to repeated Postgres TRUNCATE CASCADE NOTICE lines.
frictions.resolution: Captured full raw Vitest output in evidence logs and relied on test summary lines for outcome extraction.

blockers.description: No existing Vitest coverage for activity-driven lease renewal triggers (comment/status/keepalive) required by VAL-HARD-026.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched test and service call sites for renewLease/keepalive/comment/status-triggered renewal paths; found only direct lease-manager method tests.
